issues.comment: The comment explicitly states the intent is to "Update SelectedCalendar.updatedAt", but this passes an empty `data: {}` object to `prisma.selectedCalendar.updateMany()`. Prisma's `updateMany` does not automatically trigger `@updatedAt` — only `update()` and `upsert()` do. With `updateMany`, you must explicitly set the timestamp in the `data` argument. Passing `data: {}` will generate a SQL query with an empty `SET` clause — either a no-op or invalid. The `updatedAt` field will never be updated. Fix: Pass the timestamp explicitly: `await SelectedCalendarRepository.updateManyByCredentialId(this.credential.id, { updatedAt: new Date() });`. Impact: The feature relies on `SelectedCalendar.updatedAt` being refreshed when availability cache is rebuilt. Since it's never actually updated, the "Last updated" timestamp shown in the `CredentialActionsDropdown` UI will be stale/incorrect.
